data.body: Completed a gap analysis comparing dso's LLM code review process (~/dso_code_review_report.md, extracted from digital-service-orchestra @ 2e9eaf4a44) against rebar's four-pass code-review gate. Full report written to ~/rebar_vs_dso_code_review_gap_report.md. Headline: adopt dso's calibration CONTENT (bug-anchored FP guards, absence-claim evidence obligations, verbatim cited excerpts, behavioral-testing standard, overlay concern checklists, rationalizations-to-reject) into rebar's STRUCTURE (find->verify->decide->coach kernel, deterministic severity arithmetic), plus dso's process loop (FP ledger -> bug-anchored guard -> eval case) and cross-patchset memory built on the kernel's existing novelty axis. Key detection gaps found in rebar: test-quality anti-patterns (dso's measured #1 FP/recall class), hallucinated-reference verification, deletion/asymmetric-change sweeps, AI-blindspot patterns (ui_artifacts, spaghetti patching, domain mismatch), ticket/AC scope checking, approach-viability signal, refactor-anomaly review. Key reliability gaps: DIFF_CHAR_CAP 60k silent truncation can still vote +1 (should be a coverage-gap), no eval specs for the 4-pass code-review prompts, no FP ledger, no cross-patchset finding memory. Explicitly preserved rebar's multi-pass design (deterministic verdict, narrow Pass-2, coach-not-block, enforcement-in-types) as superior to dso's prose-governed severity; dso's stale huge-diff prompt snapshots are the cautionary tale. Report ends with a 12-item prioritized roadmap.
